title: Min Cost Climbing Stairs
description:
You are given an array `cost` where `cost[i]` is the cost of step `i`. Once you
pay, you may climb one or two steps. You can start at step `0` or step `1`.
Return the **minimum cost** to reach the top (just past the last step).

examples.explanation: Start at index 1, pay 15, and step two to the top.

solutions.summary: The cheapest way to a step is its predecessors’ best plus that step’s cost.

steps.title: Track the two previous best costs
steps.explanation: Both starting points are free, so the cost to reach step 0 and step 1 is `0`.

steps.title: Roll forward to the top
steps.explanation: Reaching the next step costs the cheaper of the last two reachable steps plus their step cost.
